results.title: In HelloNation, Masonry Expert Alan Williams Discusses the Differences of Brick & Block Walls
results.description: RICHMOND, Va. , June 10, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- What is the difference between a brick wall and a block wall? A recent HelloNation article featuring Masonry Expert Alan Williams of A. W. Masonry & Restoration explains how brick and block serve different roles in construction. The article highlights how recognizing these differences helps homeowners make informed decisions about masonry repair, maintenance, and property improvements. Brick walls have long been valued for their durability and timeless curb appeal. Many of Richmond's historic homes feature brick facades that give them their distinctive character. As the HelloNation article explains, brick offers strong resistance to weather and wear. With proper repointing and sealing, a brick wall can last for decades while maintaining its beauty. By contrast, a block wall made from concrete block provides structural support. These walls are typically larger, thicker, and stronger than brick, which makes them ideal for foundations, retaining walls, and load-bearing structures. While a brick facade enhances appearance, the block wall often serves as the hidden backbone of the building, carrying weight and resisting soil pressure. Understanding the distinction between brick and block becomes essential when planning repairs. A crumbling brick facade may only require repointing or small-scale masonry repair. But cracks in a block wall that provides structural support may indicate foundation movement, requiring more extensive intervention. Confusing the two can either lead to overlooking ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

results.title: Projected Social Security benefits cliff creeps up to 2032
results.description: By: Jennifer Shutt WASHINGTON — Congress must act to shore up Social Security during the next six years to avoid an automatic drop-off in benefits in 2032, according to a report released Tuesday. The annual update on the Old Age and Survivors Insurance trust fund shows that it will “become depleted” in the fourth quarter The post Projected Social Security benefits cliff creeps up to 2032 appeared first on KTVZ .
